author: @vengeancetechnologies1792
text:
can the average bloke get methylene chloride in the uk? used to be everywhere in the us (paint stripper) but in the last year or two all the cans of stripper advertise "new non... formula". u probably know but for those that don't.. it's the main component in the solutions for welding plexi. I've found if one cuts it with a miter saw using a bit of scrap plastic in the dispenser bottle helps control the viscosity.

I've used kapton quite a bit.. someone asked the other day whats special about it. besides yellow and non conductive I couldn't answer lol thanks!

author: @EgonSorensen
text:
Indium, Tin, and Gallium = Liquid metal - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Galinstan

replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering Subscription required - Subtotal: $40.00

author: @tasa4904
text: I wonder if you can use a cnc router to grind those layers off.  Place the glass, adjust the height, let it run across the whole surface, collect the shavings, and then repeat on the next glass pane.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you only need to do that if you want the glass whole mate - if you are doing aa ton just for the indium - crush them up

replies.author: @tasa4904
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering You made great arguments for keeping the glass and CNC machines are becoming rather cheap these days. 
That being said, I was curious whether they had the precision and work area size for this kind of task.  Otherwise we might have lots of scuffed up glass.  Better to crush the glass in that case.
